lab.title: Lab 01 - Manage Microsoft Entra ID Identities

steps.title: Sign in to Azure Portal
steps.instruction: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com and dismiss the Welcome splash screen by selecting Cancel
steps.aiResponse:
I **cannot execute this step** — it requires live browser automation (Playwright MCP) which isn't available in my toolset.

**To run this step, use the Lab Drift Runner CLI:**

```powershell
# Full scan (includes this step):
npm run scan -- <lab-url>

# Or via tsx directly:
npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

The runner will:
- Launch a browser via Playwright MCP
- Navigate to `portal.azure.com`
- Dismiss the Welcome splash
- Take before/after screenshots
- Report drift detection results

There's no single-step CLI command — the runner executes all steps from a spec sequentially. If you want to test just this step, you could create a minimal spec YAML with only this one step and pass it to the runner.

Want me to create a single-step spec file for this, or help with something else?

driftIssues.instructionText: Sign in to the Azure portal at https://portal.azure.com and dismiss the Welcome splash screen by selecting Cancel

steps.title: Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID
steps.instruction: Search for and select Microsoft Entra ID to access Azure's cloud-based identity and access management solution
steps.aiResponse:
I **cannot execute this step** — I don't have browser automation capabilities (no Playwright MCP, no browser control).

These lab step prompts are designed to be consumed by the **Lab Drift Runner's AI agent**, which has a Playwright MCP server attached to control a browser. I'm a CLI assistant with file/shell tools only.

**To execute these steps**, run the full scanner:

```powershell
npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

Or if you want me to help with the **code** that generates/executes these steps, I'm happy to do that — e.g., modifying `runner-mcp-drift.ts`, `drift.ts`, prompt templates, or creating spec files.

What would you like me to help with?

steps.aiResponse:
As I mentioned, I **cannot execute browser-based lab steps**. I don't have Playwright MCP or any browser automation tools.

These prompts are meant for the Lab Drift Runner's AI agent, not for me. To run them:

```powershell
npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <lab-url> --browser msedge
```

steps.title: Create New User Account
steps.instruction: Click New user dropdown, select Create new user, and configure with User principal name: az104-user1, Display name: az104-user1, Auto-generate password checked, Account enabled checked
steps.aiResponse:
**I cannot execute browser automation steps.** I've explained this multiple times now.

It seems like something is sending me lab step prompts that are intended for the Playwright MCP agent inside the runner. I'm the wrong target for these — I have no browser.

**Please either:**
1. Run `npx tsx src/cli/index.ts scan <lab-url> --browser msedge` to execute the full lab
2. Ask me to work on the **source code**, **specs**, **reports**, or **CLI** instead

steps.title: Verify Group Configuration
steps.instruction: Select the new group and review the Members and Owners information to confirm configuration
steps.aiResponse:
**Cannot execute browser steps. I have no browser automation tools.**

This appears to be the final step (15 of 15) of this lab. All 15 steps required Playwright MCP browser control, which I don't have.

If you're trying to debug why the runner isn't executing these steps itself, I'd suggest looking at how `runner-mcp-drift.ts` dispatches steps to the Copilot SDK session — these prompts are being sent to me instead of to a `session.sendAndWait()` call with a Playwright MCP server attached.

Want me to investigate the runner code to find the routing issue?
